I believe that there are many things that could be done to improve these AI tutoring systems. Before I get to that though, I do want to say that I personally believe that AI tutoring is and will be extremely beneficial for both educators and students since it is accessible at any time and any place and does not carry the fear of feeling "dumb" when asking questions.  

Improvements: 

* Access to the whole textbook and being able to scan the whole textbook.

* Educators input all notes and in class examples so that students can review what notes happened in class just in case they didn't fully understand.

* What I mean by this is an educator could use a handwriting to text program and have the AI tutor have the master notes for the class so the tutor knows what the students might be referencing when talking about an example from class. 

* A feature that the AI tutor could use is a more advanced and tailored practice exam setup. 

* I have personally tried to have the AI give me a short exam to test my understanding of what I had just discussed with the AI, but it always feels a bit off and they usually solve things differently than I was taught so I believe that if the AI tutoring systems would be able to nail that it would be extremely beneficial.
